## Steps to Reproduce
Proccess this code with `ktlint --format`:

```
package com.my.lint.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode

class BreakKtlintFormatRule : Rule(
    RuleId("my-ruleset:my-rule-id"),
    Rule.About(maintainer = "Me", repositoryUrl = "repositoryUrl", issueTrackerUrl = "issueTrackerUrl")
) {

    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
        node: ASTNode,
        autoCorrect: Boolean,
        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit
    ) {

        if (node.text.contains("this")
            || node.text.contains("that")
        ) {
            emit(node.startOffset, "My violation detail", false)
        }
    }
}
```

* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Command line
* Version of Gradle used (if applicable): N/A
* Operating System and version: macOS 13.2

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 13→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 14→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 15→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 16→
 17→### Changed
 18→* Separated Baseline functionality out of `ktlint-cli` into separate `ktlint-baseline` module for API consumers
 19→
 20→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 21→
 22→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 23→
 24→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 25→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 26→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 27→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 28→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 29→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 30→
 31→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 32→
 33→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 34→
 35→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 36→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 37→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 38→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 39→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 40→
 41→### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 42→
 43→The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 44→* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 45→* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
 46→* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
 47→* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
 48→* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
 49→* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
 50→* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
 51→* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
 52→* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
 53→* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
 54→* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
 55→* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
 56→
 57→Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
 58→
 59→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 60→
 61→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 62→
 63→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 64→
 65→#### Experimental rules
 66→
 67→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 68→
 69→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 70→
 71→#### EditorConfig
 72→
 73→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 74→
 75→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 76→
 77→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 78→
 79→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 80→
 81→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 82→
 83→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 84→
 85→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
 86→
 87→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 88→
 89→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 90→
 91→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 92→
 93→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 94→
 95→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
 96→
 97→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 98→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

100→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
101→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
102→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |

106→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
107→
108→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
109→
110→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |

**Evaluating null checks**

I noticed that the `isElvisOperator` function uses `firstChildNode.elementType` without a null check, leading to a potential NPE. To fix this, I should implement a safe call with `firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS`. However, I need to investigate why `firstChildNode` is null for some OPERATION_REFERENCE nodes. It could be due to an invalid parse or synthetic node issues. To avoid NPE, I'll ensure that if there’s no `firstChildNode`, it returns false, confirming it's not an Elvis operator. I'll also check other usages of `isElvisOperator` to ensure it won’t break any expectations.

content.thinking:
**Summarizing fix details**

I need to summarize the fix I made. The cause was that OPERATION_REFERENCE could be a leaf, and the firstChildNode was null, which led to an NPE due to the isElvisOperator being used without a null check. The fix involved adding a guard and checking the text as well. I should also mention that a regression test was included in the process. No git commands were involved, which is good!

content.text:
Implemented a null-safe Elvis-operator check to prevent `ktlint --format` from crashing when a line starts with `||`.

### Root cause
`IndentationRule.isElvisOperator` (and the same helper in `MultilineExpressionWrapping`) assumed that an `OPERATION_REFERENCE` node always has a `firstChildNode`. For some operators (e.g. `||`), the `OPERATION_REFERENCE` can be a leaf node, so `firstChildNode` is `null`, causing an NPE during formatting.

### Fix
- Updated `isElvisOperator` in:
  - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/.../IndentationRule.kt`
  - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/.../MultilineExpressionWrapping.kt`
- The check now safely handles leaf `OPERATION_REFERENCE` nodes:
  - Uses `firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS`
  - Falls back to `text == "?:”` to keep Elvis detection working even when the operator reference is leaf-like.

### Test
- Added a regression test (new file only):
  - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRuleRegressionOrOperatorTest.kt`
  - Verifies formatting does not crash for a multiline `if` condition where the second line starts with the logical-or operator.

 20→## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 21→
 22→WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 23→
 24→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 25→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 26→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 27→* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 28→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 29→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 30→
 31→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 32→
 33→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 34→
 35→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 36→* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 37→* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 38→* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 39→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.

 59→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 60→
 61→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 62→
 63→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 64→
 65→#### Experimental rules
 66→
 67→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 68→
 69→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 70→
 71→#### EditorConfig
 72→
 73→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 74→
 75→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 76→
 77→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 78→
 79→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 80→
 81→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
 82→
 83→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
 84→
 85→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
 86→
 87→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 88→

 90→
 91→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.

The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
